heroHeadline: Stampede Kills 30+ at Citadelle Laferrière; Election Registration Indefinitely Postponed; HRP Only ~19.5% Funded
heroSubtitle: A deadly stampede at the UNESCO-listed Citadelle Laferrière near Milot killed at least 30 on April 11–12, 2026, during a youth tourist event; the Haitian CEP indefinitely postponed voter and candidate registration on April 8, imperiling the August 30 election date; the UN warned Haiti is in 'freefall' with the 2026 HRP only ~19.5% funded ($880M needed) amid deepening USAID cuts; GSF advance force remains at ~195 personnel as the broader deployment faces funding and commitment uncertainty.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN BINUH, OHCHR, OCHA, IOM, Reuters, AP, ABC News, Miami Herald, Al Jazeera, France 24, Le Nouvelliste, ACLED, ICG, HRW, WFP, Haitian Times, Jamaica Observer, RNDDH, PassBlue, Amnesty International, IMF, HaitiLibre
